text.source_text: महत ऋग्वेदादेः शास्त्रस्य अनेक विद्यास्थानोपबृंहितस्य प्रदीपव त्सर्वार्थावद्योतिनः सर्वज्ञ कल्पस्य योनिः कारणं ब्रह्म । न ही दृशस्य शास्त्रस्य ऋग्वेदादिलक्षणस्य सर्वज्ञ गुणान्वितस्य सर्वज्ञादन्यतः सम्भवोऽस्ति । यद्य द्वि स्तरार्थं शास्त्रं यस्मात्पुरुषविशेषात्सम्भवति , यथा व्याकरणादि पाणिन्यादेः ज्ञे यैकदेशार्थमपि , स ततो ऽप्यधिकतरविज्ञान इति प्रसिद्धं लोके । किमु वक्तव्यम् — अनेक शाखा भेदभिन्नस्य देव तिर्यङ्मनुष्यवर्णाश्रमादिप्रविभागहेतोः ऋग्वेदाद्याख्यस्य सर्व ज्ञानाकरस्य अप्रयत्ने नै व लीलान्यायेन पुरुषनिःश्वासवत् यस्मान्महतो भूतात् योनेः सम्भवः — ‘ अस्य महतो भूतस्य निःश्वसितमेतत् यदृग्वेदः ’ (बृ. उ. २ । ४ । १०) इत्यादिश्रुतेः — तस्य महतो भूतस्य निरतिशयं सर्वज्ञत्वं सर्वशक्तिमत्त्वं चेति ॥
text.transliteration_iast: mahata ṛgvedādeḥ śāstrasya aneka vidyāsthānopabṛṃhitasya pradīpava tsarvārthāvadyotinaḥ sarvajña kalpasya yoniḥ kāraṇaṃ brahma | na hī dṛśasya śāstrasya ṛgvedādilakṣaṇasya sarvajña guṇānvitasya sarvajñādanyataḥ sambhavo'sti | yadya dvi starārthaṃ śāstraṃ yasmātpuruṣaviśeṣātsambhavati , yathā vyākaraṇādi pāṇinyādeḥ jñe yaikadeśārthamapi , sa tato 'pyadhikataravijñāna iti prasiddhaṃ loke | kimu vaktavyam — aneka śākhā bhedabhinnasya deva tiryaṅmanuṣyavarṇāśramādipravibhāgahetoḥ ṛgvedādyākhyasya sarva jñānākarasya aprayatne nai va līlānyāyena puruṣaniḥśvāsavat yasmānmahato bhūtāt yoneḥ sambhavaḥ — ‘ asya mahato bhūtasya niḥśvasitametat yadṛgvedaḥ ’ (bṛ. u. 2 | 4 | 10) ityādiśruteḥ — tasya mahato bhūtasya niratiśayaṃ sarvajñatvaṃ sarvaśaktimattvaṃ ceti ||

translation.literal_translation: Brahman is the source, the cause, of the great scripture beginning with the Rgveda - expanded by many seats of knowledge, illuminating all meanings like a lamp, and as if the product of omniscience. Such a scripture, marked by Rgveda etc., endowed with omniscient-grade content, cannot arise from anyone other than an omniscient source. In the world it is well known: from whatever special person a treatise of broad scope arises - even one with partial knowable range like grammar from Panini - that author is known to possess knowledge exceeding that scope. How much more here: this vast Vedic corpus, diversified into many branches and grounding distinctions of gods, animals, humans, social classes, and life-stages, treasury of all knowledge, arising effortlessly in playful manner like a breath from the Great Being - as sruti says, 'this Rgveda is the breath of that Great Being.' Therefore that Great Being possesses unsurpassed omniscience and omnipotence.
translation.technical_translation: Bhashya extends 1.1.2 with sastra-yonitva reasoning: Veda's scope and structure imply an omniscient omnipotent source, identified with Brahman.
translation.interpretive_note: Scriptural authorship/cause argument reinforces Brahman's omniscience beyond cosmological inference alone.

lexical_notes.contextual_sense: Veda’s magnitude and coherence imply an omniscient origin.

lexical_notes.gloss: source endowed with omniscience and omnipotence
lexical_notes.contextual_sense: Only such a source can ground exhaustive multi-domain revelation.
Breadth of scriptural teaching is used as evidentiary pressure for Brahman-source.
Inferential elimination of finite or inert source candidates.

lexical_notes.gloss: support for Brahman as causal source
lexical_notes.contextual_sense: Record strengthens janmadi-laksana via scripture-origin reasoning.

uncertainty_notes: Curated in contiguous S01 pass with stable inferential-tag schema.
A later philological pass should revisit janmadi-lakshana and sastra-yonitva technical compression in records 1.1.008-1.1.015.
